classification_rationale: RouteProcessor2.processRoute() has a validation-gap: it does not verify that the pool field is a legitimate Uniswap V3 pool address, so a malicious contract passed as pool receives the IUniswapV3Pool.swap() callback. Inside that callback the attacker calls uniswapV3SwapCallback() with crafted data, draining ERC-20 tokens from any user who approved RouteProcessor2. Basic swap and transfer actions follow the validation-gap callback to extract funds from victim wallets.
